trait.name: Cold-Blooded

trait.name: Serpentine
entries: The King Cobra counts as one size smaller for the purposes of moving through passageways.

trait.name: Thermal Vision
entries: The King Cobra knows the exact location of any warm-blooded creatures within 30 ft. of it.

trait.name: Venom {@recharge 5}
entries: When the Cobra hits a creature with a Strike, it can force it to make a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 11}) or it takes an extra {@damage 2d8} poison damage and is {@condition Poisoned}.
A {@condition Poisoned} creature repeats this saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On a success, this effect ends. On a failure, it takes an additional {@damage 2d8} poison damage.

On a failure, it is {@condition Charmed} and {@condition Stunned} until the start of the King Cobra's next turn. This effect ends if the creature is attacked or forced to make an ability check or saving throw.
Creatures that succeed on this saving throw are immune to the King Cobra's Hypnotic Gaze for the next 24 hours.

trait.name: Ambush Hunter

trait.name: Limited Flight

trait.name: Serrated Grasp

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 2d4 + 3}) piercing damage.

A {@condition Poisoned} creature repeats this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. On a failure, it takes {@damage 1d8} poison damage. If this reduces it to 0 hit points, it is stable, but {@condition Paralyzed}, while at 0 hit points.

trait.name: Swallow
entries: When the Titanoboa uses its Strike on a creature {@condition Grappled} by it, it Swallows it. A Swallowed creature is {@condition Blinded} and {@condition Restrained}, and it has total cover from attacks and effects from outside of the Titanoboa.

At the start of the Titanoboa's turn, Swallowed creatures take ({@dice 5d4}) acid damage, then Titanoboa must make a Constitution saving throw (DC is the total damage taken since its previous turn). On a failure, any Swallowed creatures land {@condition Prone} in an unoccupied space within 5 ft. of the Titanoboa.

action.name: Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 2d10 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 2d12 + 6}) bludgeoning damage, and if the target is equal to the Anaconda's size (or smaller) it is {@condition Grappled} (escape {@dc 17}) and {@condition Restrained}. The Anaconda can only {@action Grapple} one creature at a time in this way.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The Badger has advantage on ability checks that realy on its sense of smell.

trait.name: Strong Jaws
entries: If the Badger hits a creature equal to its size (or smaller) with Bite, it can {@action Grapple} it in its jaws (escape {@dc 17}), but then the Badger cannot Bite other targets.

trait.name: Thick Hide
entries: The Badger reduces all damage, other than force, psychic, and radiant by its Con. modifier (4).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Badger makes two attacks, using its Bite and Claw attacks in any combination.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 3d6 + 6}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 3d8 + 6}) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Aggressive
entries: The Badger takes the {@action Dash} action, but must end its movement closer to a Hostile creature.

bonus.name: Frenzy
entries: The Badger enters a Frenzy, and until the start of its next turn, it has Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and all of the Badger's attacks and all attacks against the Badger. are made with advantage.

trait.name: Imposing Presence
entries: The Bear uses its Strength, in place of Charisma, for {@skill Intimidation} checks (included above).

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The Cave Bear has advantage on all ability checks that rely on its sense of smell.

trait.name: Strong Jaws
entries: If the Bear hits a creature equal to its size (or smaller) with its Bite, it can {@action Grapple} it in its jaws (escape {@dc 18}), but the Bear can't use its Bite on other targets.

trait.name: Thick Hide
entries: The Cave Bear reduces damage, other than force, psychic, and radiant by its Constitution mod. (5).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Bear makes two attacks, using its Bite and its Claw attacks in any combination.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 2d10 + 7}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 3d8 + 7}) slashing damage, and any creature equal to the Bear's size (or smaller) is knocked {@condition Prone}.

bonus.name: Roar of Dominance
entries: After the Cave Bear hits a creature its Bite or Claw attack, it can roar, and force the creature hit by its attack to make a Wisdom saving throw ({@dc 18}). On a failed save, it becomes {@condition Frightened} of the Cave Bear until the start of the Cave Bear's next turn.
Creatures that succeed on this saving throw are immune to the Bear's Roar of Dominance for the next 24 hours.

trait.name: Aquatic Camouflage
entries: While it remains motionless in the water, the Crocodile is indistinguishable from its natural surroundings due to the algae buildup on its scales.

trait.name: Crushing Strength
entries: If the Crocodile damages a creature while it is holding its breath underwater, it knocks the air from its lungs, and the creature begins Suffocating.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The Crocodile can hold breath for 3 hours.

trait.name: Strong Jaws
entries: If the Crocodile hits a target equal to its size (or smaller) with Bite, it can {@action Grapple} it in its jaws (escape {@dc 18}), but the Crocodile cannot Bite other targets.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Crocodile makes two attacks, a Bite and a Tail attack. It can replace its Bite attack with Death Roll.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 4d10 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Death Roll
entries: The Crocodile forces one creature {@condition Grappled} by it to make a Strength saving throw ({@dc 16}). It takes ({@dice 4d10}) slashing damage on failure, and half on success.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 2d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and if it is equal to the Crocodile's size (or smaller) it falls {@condition Prone}.

trait.name: Concussive Blow
entries: If the Gorilla hits a creature smaller than it with a Fist, the target must make a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 17}) or the be {@condition Stunned} until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Imposing Presence
entries: The Gorilla uses its Strength, in place of Charisma, for {@skill Intimidation} checks (included above).

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The Gorilla has advantage on all ability checks that rely on its sense of smell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Gorilla makes two attacks, using Fist or Hurl.

action.name: Fist
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 3d8 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Hurl
entries: {@atk rw} The Gorilla throws an object or Medium (or smaller) creature {@condition Grappled} by it. {@hit 9} to hit, range 30/60 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 2d10 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Frenzy
entries: The Gorilla enters a Frenzy, and until the start of its next turn, it has {@spell Resistance} to all Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and all of the Gorilla's attacks and attacks against the Gorilla are made with advantage.

bonus.name: Intimidating Show
entries: The Gorilla forces Hostile creatures within 30 feet to make a Wisdom saving throw ({@dc 17}), or become {@condition Frightened} of it for 1 minute. Creatures can repeat this saving throw at the end of their turn, ending the effect on a success.
Creatures that succeed on this saving throw are immune to the Gorilla's Indimidating Show for the next 24 hours.

trait.name: Concussive Blow
entries: If the Mamoth hits a creature smaller than it with its Stomp attack, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 18}). On a failed save, it is {@condition Stunned} until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Imposing Presence
entries: The Mammoth uses its Strength, in place of Charisma, for {@skill Intimidation} checks (see above).

trait.name: Thick Hide
entries: The Mammoth reduces damage, other than force, psychic, and radiant by its Constitution mod. (5).

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the Mammoth moves at least 20 ft. in a line and hits with Gore, it deals a bonus {@damage 1d8} damage. If the target is equal to the Mammoth's size (or smaller), the target is also knocked {@condition Prone}, and the Mammoth can one Stomp attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Gore
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 4d8 + 7}) piercing damage.

action.name: Stomp
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 4d10 + 7}) bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Aquatic
entries: The Shark can only breathe underwater.

trait.name: Blood Sense
entries: The Shark knows the direction of any creature with less than its maximum hit points within 1 mile, so long as they are in the same body of water.

trait.name: Frenzy
entries: The Shark has advantage on Bite attacks against creatures with less than their maximum hit points.

trait.name: Relentless {@recharge 4}
entries: If the Shark is reduced to 0 points, but not killed, it falls to 11 ({@dice 1d12 + 5}) hit points.

trait.name: Swallow
entries: Any creature that is Swallowed by the Shark is {@condition Blinded} and {@condition Restrained}, and has total cover from attacks and effects from outside of the Shark.

At the start of the Shark's turns, Swallowed creatures take ({@dice 6d4}) acid damage, then Shark must make a Constitution saving throw (DC equals the total damage taken since its previous turn). On a failed save, Swallowed creatures land {@condition Prone} in an unoccupied space within 5 ft. of the Shark.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 5d10 + 6}) piercing damage, and a target that is two sizes smaller than it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw ({@dc 17}) or be Swallowed.

bonus.name: Aggressive
entries: The Shark takes the {@action Dash} action, but it must end its movement closer to a Hostile creature.

trait.name: Airborne Evasion
entries: When the Eagle is forced to make a Dexterity saving throw to avoid damage while flying, it takes no damage on a success, and half on a failure.

trait.name: Hunter's Grasp
entries: If the Eagle hits a target at least one size smaller than it with its Talons, it can {@action Grapple} it (escape {@dc 17}), then it cannot use its Talons on other targets.

trait.name: Hunter's Senses
entries: The Eagle adds double its Proficiency Bonus to its {@skill Perception} checks (included above).

trait.name: Swift Wing
entries: {@action opportunity attack||Opportunity attacks} against the Eagle have disadvantage while it is using its flying speed.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Eagle uses its Screech (if available), then makes two Beak or Talons attacks in any combination.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 2d8 + 6}) piercing damage, and the creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw ({@dc 17}) or it is {@condition Blinded} until the start of the Eagle's next turn.

action.name: Talons
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 2d10 + 6}) slashing damage, and the target has disadvantage on attack rolls against all targets other than the Eagle until the start of the Eagle's next turn.

action.name: Screech {@recharge 4}
entries: The Eagle forces hostile targets that can see or hear it within 120 ft. to make a Wisdom saving throw ({@dc 17}). On a failure, they are {@condition Frightened} of the Eagle for 1 minute. They repeat the saving throw at the end of their turn, ending the effect on a success.
Creatures that succeed on this Wisdom saving throw are immune to the Eagle's Screech for the next 24 hours.

trait.name: Arachnid
entries: The Spider can climb on sheer surfaces, upside down, and along webs without making an ability check. It also ignores movement restrictions caused by webs and it can sense other creatures touching the same web as it.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The Spider's attacks deal the maximum damage, in place of rolling, against objects and boats.

trait.name: Venom {@recharge 4}
entries: If the Spider hits with its Bite, it can force the target to make a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 17}). On a failure, it takes {@damage 4d8} poison damage and is {@condition Poisoned}. On a success, it only takes half damage.

A {@condition Poisoned} creature repeats this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. On a failure, it takes {@damage 2d8} poison damage. If this reduces it to 0 hit points, it is stable, but {@condition Paralyzed}, while at 0 hit points.

trait.name: Wrap
entries: When the Spider targets a creature equal to its size (or smaller) with its Web Strand, it must make a Dexterity saving throw ({@dc 17}) or be wrapped in webs that reduce its speed to 0. As an action it can make a Strength check, destroying the webs and escaping on a success.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 5d8 + 6}) piercing damage.

action.name: Web Strand
entries: The Spider shoots a thin strand of webbing at a target it can see within 60 ft., forming a continuous line of webbing from where it is standing to the target.

trait.name: Echolocation
entries: If the Whale makes a {@skill Perception} check with its hearing it treats a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10. It loses this benefit and its {@sense Blindsight} if it is {@condition Deafened}.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The Whale's attacks deal maximum damage, in place of rolling, against objects and boats.

trait.name: Thick Hide
entries: The Whale reduces all damage, other than force, psychic, and radiant by its Con. modifier (5).

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The Whale can hold its breath for 2 hours.

trait.name: Swallow
entries: Any creature that is Swallowed by the Whale is {@condition Blinded} and {@condition Restrained}, and has total cover from attacks and effects from outside of the Whale.

At the start of the Whale's turns, Swallowed creatures take ({@dice 6d4}) acid damage, then Whale must make a Constitution saving throw (DC equals the total damage taken since its previous turn). On a failed save, Swallowed creatures land {@condition Prone} in an unoccupied space within 5 ft. of the Whale.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Whale makes two attacks using its Bite and Tail attacks in any combination.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 3d8 + 8}) piercing damage, and if the target is two sizes smaller than the Whale, it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw ({@dc 19}) or it is Swallowed.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}({@dice 3d6 + 8}) bludgeoning damage, and a target equal to the Whale's size (or smaller) is knocked {@condition Prone}.

monsterFluff.name: CR 0
entries: Beasts of Challenge Rating 0 include the most common and plentiful Beasts that exist on the Material Plane. Beasts of CR 0 are not a threat unless encountered in large groups.

When a creature casts the {@spell conjure familiar|llspell} spell, they can also select {@filter any CR 0 Beast from this Compendium|bestiary|source=llcompendiumofbeasts|Challenge Rating=[&0]|type=beast}, in addition to the normal options included with the {@spell conjure familiar|llspell} spell.

items.name: Reskinning Beast Statblocks
entries: Many of the stat blocks in this Compendium can be used to represent multiple Beasts.
For example, the {@creature Raven|LLCompendiumOfBeasts} stat block can be used for songbirds, parrots, or other birds.
Similarly, the {@creature Rodent|LLCompendiumOfBeasts} stat block can be used to represent the multitude of small furry mammals: mice, rats, squirrels, hedgehogs, ferrets, etc.

monsterFluff.name: CR 1
entries: Beasts of Challenge Rating 1 are apex predators or massive herd animals. Beasts of this CR make formidable enemies by themselves for a group of inexperienced adventurers.

monsterFluff.name: CR 2
entries: Beasts of Challenge Rating 2 are highly dangerous monsters which inspire myth and legend amongst the common people. These Beasts are deadly to all but the most skilled hunters.

monsterFluff.name: CR 3
entries: Beasts of Challenge Rating 3 are massive and extremely rare Beasts which dominate their chosen habitat. When commonfolk venture into their lairs they are never heard from again.

monsterFluff.name: CR 4
entries: Beasts of Challenge Rating 4 inspire myth and legend when travelers tell tales about them. These Beasts can challenge the most skilled local hunters and adventuring parties.

monsterFluff.name: CR 5
entries: Beasts of Challenge Rating 5 are titans of the natural world and often embody the destructive and wild wrath of nature.

monsterFluff.name: CR 6
entries: Beasts of Challenge Rating 6 sit at the apex of the animal kingdom. They are the most powerful Beasts that roam the wilds short of those empowered by primal forces or magic.

monsterFluff.name: CR ¼
entries: Beasts of Challenge Rating ¼ include large and potentially dangerous herbivores, along with small predators which are deadly to common folk, and pose a risk to adventurers.

monsterFluff.name: CR ½
entries: Beasts of Challenge Rating ½ include the most powerful domesticated animals and deadly hunters in their habitat.

monsterFluff.name: CR ⅛
entries: Beasts of Challenge Rating ⅛ include common Beasts that are hardy, trainable by humanoids, or potentially dangerous should they catch a group of adventurers unaware.

items: {@i Legends tell of a powerful fey creature spoken of in whispers as the {@creature Laser Llama|LLLaserLlama} {@note ({@link Original URL|https://www.gmbinder.com/share/-O7o2cNFhle7UKgXAMkk})}. The legends say this mythical llama can fly and conjure blasts of pure radiance to smite its foes...}

variantrule.name: New Rules for Beasts
entries: Multiple creatures included in the {@book laserllama compendium of Beasts|LLCompendiumOfBeasts} use the new and alternate rules detailed below:

entries.name: Adjusted Challenge Ratings
entries: {@variantrule Challenge Rating|PHB|Challenge Ratings (CR)} have been updated to reflect the threat that such a Beast would pose to four adventurers of that level.

entries.name: Beast Subtypes
entries: Like other types of monsters (notably fiends), the Beasts here now each have one of the following subtypes: {@i Aquatic, Avian,Brute, Carnivore, Reptilian,} or {@i Vermin}.
Though, legends speak of extinct Beasts that might exist in the remote parts of the wild world with the {@i Ancient} subtype.

entries.name: Recharge Features
entries: If you use a feature to take on the shape of a creature with a Recharge feature (either on a {@dice d6} roll of 5–6 or after a short or long rest), you do not regain the use of that feature until thespecified recharge condition is met, even if you revert to yournormal form then take on the shape of that creature again.

entries.name: Size: Diminutive
entries: Diminutive is a new size category for creatures smaller than Tiny. Diminutive creatures are smaller than 1 foot × 1 foot, and include vermin, rodents, insects, and other small creatures.

If you use a feature to take on the shape of a Diminutive creature, such as the {@spell polymorph|}{@sup LL} spell or the {@class Druid|PHB|Druid's} {@classFeature Wild Shape|Druid|PHB|2}, you immediately revert to your normal form when you take any amount of damage or when you fail a saving throw.
{@style Unleash Wild Fury with this Compendium of Alternate Beasts for the World's Greatest Roleplaying Game|dnd-font;large}

The Beasts included here have been redesigned to be more dynamic in play.
